alt: Snow-covered Clarence Island in the South Shetland archipelago rising from dark Southern Ocean water under a cap-cloud, with a pale-blue iceberg drifting alongside the island's eastern shore
description: Clarence Island, the easternmost island of the South Shetland archipelago, viewed from the sea — snow-covered mountainous summit with characteristic cap-cloud (orographic), a pale-blue iceberg drifting in the foreground, and dark Southern Ocean water. The South Shetland Islands sit at ~62°S, about 120 km north of the Antarctic Peninsula.
title: Clarence Island, Antarctica
